---
name: review-frontend-flow
description: Review a frontend page, screen, component flow, or UI diff for interaction quality, state completeness, form behavior, and frontend/backend alignment. Use when Codex should evaluate whether a user-facing flow is truly ready, not just visually present.
---

# review-frontend-flow

Review the full user flow, not just the rendered surface.

## Workflow

1. Reconstruct the intended user task and main path.
2. Inspect the changed UI structure, event flow, and data dependencies.
3. Check state completeness: initial, loading, empty, success, error, retry, and permission-limited states when relevant.
4. Check form behavior when the flow collects or mutates user input.
5. Check whether backend responses, errors, and permissions support what the UI claims.
6. Separate must-fix interaction issues from polish and consistency improvements.
7. Conclude with a practical readiness judgment.

## Output

Always include:

- Flow summary
- Must fix
- Should improve
- State gaps
- Contract or backend assumptions
- Readiness judgment

## Review standard

- Prefer user-task failure modes over abstract frontend criticism.
- Treat missing states as real defects when they can trap or confuse users.
- Distinguish interaction breakage from visual polish.
- If the flow is solid, say so clearly instead of inventing issues.

## References

- Read `../../packs/frontend-state-flow-pack.md` for page and mutation-state coverage.
- Read `../../packs/form-flow-pack.md` when the flow includes input, submission, settings, or modal forms.
- Read `../../packs/fullstack-contract-pack.md` when frontend behavior depends on backend field shape, status values, or permission behavior.
- Read `../../packs/release-risk-pack.md` when deciding whether the flow is safe to ship.
- Read `../../packs/component-consistency-pack.md` when repeated UI patterns, visual hierarchy, or design-system consistency are part of the review.
- Read `../../packs/nextjs-app-pack.md` when the flow is implemented in Next.js and depends on App Router, server components, or route-state behavior.
- Read `../../packs/trpc-pack.md` when the flow depends on tRPC procedures, query invalidation, mutation reconciliation, or client/server type contracts.
- Read `../design-review/references/ux-checklist.md` for general usability checks.
- Read `../design-review/references/ai-slop-signals.md` when the UI feels autogenerated, weakly integrated, or visually inconsistent.
- Read `references/readiness-template.md` when a more formal frontend review report is useful.
